source_jobs.title: Legal Operations and Innovation @ Clay
source_jobs.description:
EMPOWERING CREATIVE GROWTH FOR MODERN ORGANIZATIONS


BUILDING SCALABLE SYSTEMS FOR LEGAL EXCELLENCE

Clay Labs is seeking a Legal Operations leader to architect and drive the next phase of its legal function. As the company accelerates growth and complexity, this role will transform Legal from a reactive support team into a strategic enabler across the business.

The Legal Operations role is foundational—responsible for designing and implementing the systems, processes, and infrastructure that will support Clay’s expanding customer base, product suite, and cross-functional teams. This is a builder’s opportunity: you will create legal workflows, manage intake and prioritization, and ensure seamless collaboration with Sales, Customer Success, Support, and Finance. You will own relationships with external counsel and vendors, manage the legal tech stack (including Ironclad and Docusign), and leverage AI to automate and optimize legal processes.

Success in this role requires experience in legal operations or program management, a strong track record of building scalable solutions, and deep familiarity with legal technology and AI-driven workflows. The ideal candidate thrives in fast-paced, high-growth environments and brings sharp analytical skills, operational rigor, and a collaborative mindset.

Company Website: https://clay.com"

source_jobs.title: General Counsel - Corporate and Securities @ Host Digital
source_jobs.description:
GUIDING CORPORATE GROWTH WITH LEGAL EXCELLENCE


SHAPING GOVERNANCE AND COMPLIANCE FOR A DYNAMIC PUBLIC COMPANY

The company is seeking an accomplished General Counsel – Corporate & Securities to serve as its top legal executive and strategic advisor to the CEO and Board of Directors. As the first senior legal hire, this leader will establish and oversee the legal function, guiding all aspects of corporate governance, securities compliance, and risk management for a fast-paced, entrepreneurial public company.

This role is responsible for advising executive management and the Board on fiduciary duties, governance, and regulatory requirements, while maintaining corporate records and supporting shareholder communications. The General Counsel will lead all public company legal and securities compliance matters, including SEC filings, disclosure obligations, and insider trading policies, ensuring strict adherence to regulatory standards.

The position oversees legal execution of capital markets and strategic transactions, such as public and private securities offerings, mergers, acquisitions, and financings. The General Counsel will also draft and negotiate a wide range of commercial agreements, develop standardized legal processes, and advise on contractual risk and commercial strategy.

Candidates with experience in commercial real estate, infrastructure development, or project finance will be strongly considered, though this is not required. The role also encompasses corporate compliance, litigation management, intellectual property, privacy, and employment law matters.

The ideal candidate holds a J.D., is an active member of a U.S. state bar, and brings at least 8 years of relevant legal experience, including significant expertise in corporate and securities law for publicly traded companies. Strong communication, negotiation, and leadership skills are essential, along with the ability to operate independently and collaboratively in a dynamic environment.

source_jobs.title: General Counsel @ Zanskar
source_jobs.description:
BUILDING THE FUTURE OF CARBON-FREE BASELOAD ENERGY


COMBINING AI AND GEOTHERMAL INFRASTRUCTURE TO DECARBONIZE THE GRID

Zanskar is developing the next generation of geothermal infrastructure by combining AI-powered resource discovery with large-scale energy development. The company identifies, develops, and operates geothermal power assets designed to deliver reliable, carbon-free baseload energy at a time when global demand for clean infrastructure is accelerating.

Zanskar is hiring a General Counsel to build and lead its legal function during a critical phase of company growth. Reporting directly to the CEO, this role serves as both a strategic executive partner and the architect of a modern, high-leverage legal organization designed to support rapid infrastructure development and long-term scale.

As the company’s first General Counsel, you will oversee legal strategy across project development, financing, corporate matters, regulatory issues, public policy, HR, and real property. A core focus of the role is ensuring that development and construction activities are contractually structured to meet the standards required for institutional financing and project bankability.

The role also involves navigating the complex regulatory and permitting landscape tied to geothermal development, including mineral rights, surface rights, and federal, state, and local permitting frameworks. You will work closely with leadership to evaluate legal risk in the context of ambitious commercial and operational objectives, helping the business move quickly while maintaining strong legal and strategic discipline.

In parallel, you will build the company’s legal infrastructure from the ground up. This includes managing a broad network of outside counsel across specialized domains while designing scalable internal systems and workflows. Zanskar is particularly interested in a leader who embraces AI and automation as core tools for creating a lean, future-oriented legal department capable of operating with high efficiency and leverage.

The ideal candidate brings significant experience within energy, infrastructure, project development, or related sectors, particularly in areas involving project finance, permitting, and complex commercial execution. Equally important is a builder mentality—the ability to operate effectively in ambiguity, create structure from scratch, and serve as a pragmatic business partner in a fast-moving environment.

This is a foundational executive role for a lawyer who wants to help shape both a company and an emerging category within the clean energy transition.

Company Website: zanskar.us http://zanskar.us

source_jobs.title: Experienced Crypto Attorney - Contract Basis @ Pareto Law Group
source_jobs.description:
BOUTIQUE TECHNOLOGY LAW FIRM: SUPPORTING THE NEXT GENERATION OF HARD TECH AND CRYPTO COMPANIES
EXPANDING SPECIALIZED LEGAL SUPPORT FOR EMERGING DIGITAL ASSET BUSINESSES

A boutique law firm focused on early-stage technology companies, particularly in the hard tech sector, is seeking a Contract Crypto Counsel to support a growing number of client opportunities in the digital assets space.

The firm works closely with founders and emerging technology companies and has strong relationships with industry leaders in crypto. As inbound demand increases, the firm is looking for an experienced attorney who can provide practical, commercially minded advice on crypto, blockchain, and related regulatory matters.

The Contract Crypto Counsel will support clients on a project-by-project basis, advising on issues such as digital asset regulation, securities and commodities laws, money transmission, payments, token-related matters, compliance, commercial agreements, and product counseling. The role may also involve supporting financings, corporate transactions, and other legal needs as client matters evolve.

The ideal candidate is an active member of at least one U.S. state bar and has meaningful experience advising crypto, blockchain, fintech, or digital asset companies. Strong regulatory knowledge, sound business judgment, and the ability to work independently with founders and senior executives are essential.

Work is expected to be flexible and intermittent, making this opportunity well suited for an experienced attorney seeking fractional, contract, or moonlighting work.

source_jobs.title: Senior Counsel, Regulatory @ Headway
source_jobs.description:
BUILDING A NEW ERA OF ACCESSIBLE MENTAL HEALTHCARE


EMPOWERING PROVIDERS AND PATIENTS THROUGH INNOVATIVE TECHNOLOGY

Headway is seeking a Senior Counsel, Regulatory to serve as a strategic legal partner during a pivotal phase of growth. This role is central to Headway’s mission of transforming mental healthcare access by supporting the development of technology that enables over 75,000 providers to serve more than one million patients nationwide.

As Senior Counsel, Regulatory, you will collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, Operations, and Privacy teams, providing real-time guidance on clinical practice, telehealth, and regulatory implications for product development. You will act as the primary regulatory attorney for Clinical and Operations teams, translating complex healthcare regulations into actionable guidance and tools. Your expertise will help anticipate regulatory challenges, develop proactive solutions, and position legal guidance as a business enabler.

Key responsibilities include partnering on policy development, audit response, and remediation strategies, as well as building strong cross-functional relationships to support Headway’s mission-driven work. The ideal candidate brings significant healthcare regulatory experience, particularly in health technology, corporate practice of medicine, and behavioral health, with a proven ability to balance compliance and business objectives in a fast-paced environment.

Company Website: https://headway.co

source_jobs.title: Legal Counsel @ Geotab
source_jobs.description:
DRIVING THE FUTURE OF CONNECTED TRANSPORTATION


EMPOWERING BUSINESSES WITH DATA-DRIVEN FLEET INTELLIGENCE

Geotab is seeking a Legal Counsel to manage complex legal agreements and negotiations across technology, data licensing, government contracting, privacy, and intellectual property. As a global leader in IoT and connected transportation, Geotab processes billions of data points daily to help organizations optimize fleets, enhance safety, and ensure regulatory compliance.

In this role, you will oversee a broad portfolio of legal matters, including end user, distribution, OEM, and wireless carrier agreements. You will support negotiations with a diverse range of stakeholders—resellers, customers, OEMs, suppliers, and government entities—integrating legal, technical, and business considerations to deliver practical solutions. The position also involves contributing to legal strategies for mass contracting, product development, risk management, and emerging issues in AI, big data, and privacy.

You will provide legal support for product design, intellectual property litigation, M&A activity, and global expansion initiatives. The ideal candidate brings 5-8 years of experience in a law firm or in-house legal department, is licensed to practice in Ontario, and has a strong background in technology transactions, government contracting, and data privacy. Experience with AI tools, software, and technology-focused joint ventures is highly valued, along with strong analytical, negotiation, and project management skills.

Geotab values ethics, diplomacy, and the ability to manage sensitive information with discretion. The company fosters a collaborative environment where legal counsel plays a key role in supporting innovation and strategic growth.

Company Website: https://www.geotab.com

source_jobs.title: Counsel, Commercial and Compliance @ Curri
source_jobs.description:
DELIVERING THE FUTURE OF CONSTRUCTION LOGISTICS


BUILDING THE PLATFORM FOR ON-DEMAND INDUSTRIAL SUPPLY DELIVERY

Curri is seeking an Assistant General Counsel to join its legal team as the second attorney and primary partner to the Chief Legal Officer. This is a broad, high-impact generalist role with real ownership across commercial, compliance, employment, regulatory, and strategic matters from day one. The Assistant General Counsel will design and implement AI-enabled legal workflows, manage commercial contracting processes, develop compliance programs, and serve as a trusted advisor to teams across the business.

Reporting directly to the CLO, this role is central to shaping Curri’s legal function and supporting the company’s mission to transform last-mile logistics for construction and industrial supplies. The ideal candidate brings 4–9 years of experience in both law firm and/or in-house settings, a proven ability to operate across multiple legal domains, and hands-on experience building or operating AI-enabled legal workflows. Strong legal operations skills, clear communication with non-legal stakeholders, and a self-directed, ownership-oriented approach are essential.

This opportunity offers direct influence over how the legal function is built, genuine autonomy, and the chance to work in a high-growth, mission-driven environment where legal is embedded as a working partner to the business.

Learn more at curri.com http://curri.com

source_jobs.title: Senior Legal Counsel - Corporate and Securities @ BetterUp
source_jobs.description:
BUILDING THE PERMANENT HOME FOR ESSENTIAL SOFTWARE


EMPOWERING AMBITIOUS BUSINESSES WITH LONG-TERM GROWTH

Beacon Software is seeking an M&A Counsel to lead the legal execution of mergers and acquisitions across its expanding portfolio of mission-critical software businesses. As a permanent capital holding company, Beacon acquires and grows essential businesses, supporting founders and teams with deep operational, technical, and investment expertise.

In this high-impact role, the M&A Counsel will serve as the primary legal lead on transactions from LOI through post-closing, reporting to the VP, Legal. Responsibilities include managing all legal work streams, conducting in-house due diligence, drafting and negotiating transaction documents, and ensuring seamless deal execution across multiple concurrent transactions. The role requires direct partnership with Beacon’s investment and operations teams, providing real-time legal guidance and risk assessment, and leveraging AI tools to drive efficiency throughout the deal lifecycle.

The ideal candidate brings 3–5 years of M&A experience at a top-tier law firm or in-house, with a proven ability to independently run transactions end-to-end. Strong proficiency with AI-enabled legal tools, broad knowledge of U.S. and/or Canadian M&A frameworks, and the ability to address general legal issues across disciplines are essential. Experience with technology-focused or SaaS M&A is a strong plus.

Beacon offers a unique environment combining the pace of a high-growth technology company with the stability of a long-term investment firm. Team members benefit from mentorship, exposure to diverse industries, and the opportunity to work alongside entrepreneurs and investors who have scaled businesses from startup to IPO.

Learn more at: https://beacon.io

source_jobs.title: Employment & Compliance Counsel @ Human Interest
source_jobs.description:
ENSURING FINANCIAL SECURITY FOR ALL


TRANSFORMING ACCESS TO RETIREMENT BENEFITS FOR AMERICAN WORKERS

Human Interest is seeking an experienced Employment & Compliance Counsel to join its Legal team. This pivotal role supports Human Interest’s mission to make retirement savings accessible and affordable for employees of small and medium-sized businesses nationwide.

As Employment & Compliance Counsel, you will serve as the primary legal advisor to the People Team on all aspects of employment law, including hiring, performance management, terminations, and sensitive employee matters. You will provide proactive counsel on employment law compliance, with particular depth advising on non-exempt employee populations, and support internal investigations, policy development, and employment documentation.

In addition to employment counseling, you will provide broad legal support across the business, including regulatory investigations, privacy and information security matters, litigation support, and contract review. This dual-mandate role requires a strategic partner who can translate complex legal requirements into practical guidance for business stakeholders in a fast-paced, regulated fintech environment.

The ideal candidate brings 8–10+ years of legal experience, including significant employment law counseling and at least one prior in-house legal role. A law firm background and experience in high-growth technology companies are strong assets. Demonstrated fluency with AI tools and a commitment to leveraging technology for legal work are essential.

This is an opportunity to help shape the future of retirement by ensuring legal integrity and supporting innovation at scale. Human Interest values curiosity, process rigor, and a collaborative spirit—qualities that will enable you to thrive and make a lasting impact.

Company Website: https://humaninterest.com

source_jobs.title: Associate General Counsel @ Stedi
source_jobs.description:
BUILDING THE FUTURE OF HEALTHCARE INFRASTRUCTURE


MODERNIZING MISSION-CRITICAL HEALTHCARE TRANSACTIONS

Stedi is seeking an Associate General Counsel to help shape the legal foundation of the first new healthcare clearinghouse in decades. As the second attorney on a lean, high-performing team, this role offers broad ownership across complex legal matters in a highly regulated and rapidly evolving sector.

The Associate General Counsel will partner closely with the Head of Legal and collaborate with company leadership and teams across the business. Responsibilities include managing customer and vendor contracting, driving payer trading partner agreements, serving as Product Counsel for innovative engineering teams, and improving legal workflows and automation. The role requires strong healthcare experience, excellent judgment, and the ability to translate legal expertise into business strategy.

Ideal candidates are bar-admitted, experienced in healthcare law (BigLaw or in-house), and comfortable navigating regulatory and commercial complexity. The position demands clear communication, high-volume work management, and the ability to create scalable processes in a fast-paced environment. Stedi values resourcefulness, precision, and a relentless drive to deliver results with professionalism and integrity.

Company Website: stedi.com http://stedi.com

source_jobs.title: Assistant General Counsel @ General Matter
source_jobs.description:
RESTORING AMERICA’S NUCLEAR FUEL INDEPENDENCE


POWERING CRITICAL INDUSTRIES WITH NEXT-GENERATION URANIUM ENRICHMENT

General Matter is seeking a senior Compliance Attorney to help build and lead its legal compliance function at a pivotal moment for the company and the nation. As the first compliance-focused attorney, you will work closely with the General Counsel to design and implement compliance strategies that enable business growth while minimizing risk across one of the most heavily regulated industries in America.

This high-impact role offers significant autonomy and responsibility, with opportunities to collaborate directly with business and technical leaders. You will address complex issues spanning export controls, classified information, environmental, health, safety, and nuclear licensing. The position requires tactical judgment, strong interpersonal skills, and deep subject matter expertise. Depending on experience, the role may be structured as Deputy or Associate General Counsel.

Ideal candidates will have a J.D. and at least 8 years of legal experience, including substantial expertise in export controls, classification, and/or nuclear compliance. This is an onsite, full-time position based in Los Angeles.

Company Website: https://generalmatter.com

source_jobs.title: Deputy General Counsel @ 1Money
source_jobs.description:
ENABLING SECURE DIGITAL ASSET TRANSACTIONS


BUILDING TRUST IN THE EVOLVING WORLD OF VIRTUAL CURRENCIES

1Money is seeking a Deputy General Counsel to join its legal team and help navigate the complex regulatory landscape of digital assets and money services. As a FinCEN-registered Money Services Business operating across the United States and a licensed digital asset business in Bermuda, 1Money delivers regulated products and services through a robust compliance framework.

In this role, the Deputy General Counsel will play a critical part in advising on legal and regulatory matters related to digital assets, stablecoins, and money transmission. The position involves supporting the company’s licensing, compliance, and risk management efforts, ensuring adherence to all applicable laws and regulations. The successful candidate will work closely with cross-functional teams to address legal risks, manage disclosures, and uphold customer protections in a rapidly changing environment. This is a high-impact opportunity to shape legal strategy at the intersection of financial technology and digital assets.

Company Website: https://1money.com

source_jobs.title: Director, Commercial Legal @ Remote Technology
source_jobs.description:
ENABLING GLOBAL TEAMS, SEAMLESSLY


REDEFINING THE FUTURE OF WORK WITH TRUSTED HR TECHNOLOGY

Remote is seeking a Director, Commercial Legal to lead its Commercial Legal function and support the company’s mission to make global employment simple, compliant, and accessible for organizations everywhere. As a senior member of the Legal team, this role is responsible for guiding a talented group of commercial lawyers and delivering pragmatic, innovative legal solutions that empower Remote’s growth in the HR-tech and SaaS sectors.

The Director, Commercial Legal will serve as the directly responsible individual for all commercial legal matters, overseeing contract reviews and negotiations for sales, partnerships, and procurement. This leader will drive improvements in commercial terms and contracting processes, enabling go-to-market teams to close enterprise-level deals efficiently. The role also provides strategic support to partnership teams, ensuring business intent is captured while maintaining commercial viability and timely deal closure.

Collaboration is central to this position. The Director will work closely with revenue operations and legal operations to automate workflows, leverage AI for contracting efficiency, and maintain the contract lifecycle management system. Building strong relationships with stakeholders across GTM, Finance, and other teams is essential to inform decision-making and enhance Legal’s influence in commercial strategy.

The ideal candidate is a qualified lawyer with significant in-house experience in commercial contracts, particularly within HR-tech or SaaS environments. Demonstrated leadership in managing legal teams, a track record of complex negotiations, and a passion for using technology and AI to drive efficiency are critical. The ability to mentor, motivate, and build trusted relationships in a fully remote, asynchronous environment is key.

remote.com http://remote.com

source_jobs.title: Senior Counsel, Commercial @ Poshmark
source_jobs.description:
EMPOWERING A VIBRANT FASHION COMMUNITY


ENABLING CONNECTION, DISCOVERY, AND SELF-EXPRESSION THROUGH COMMERCE

Poshmark is seeking a Senior Counsel, Commercial to join its core Legal team and serve as a trusted advisor across the organization. Reporting to the Head of Legal, this role leads the commercial legal function, driving the structuring, negotiation, and execution of a wide range of commercial agreements—including partnerships, technology, professional services, marketing, and vendor contracts. The Senior Counsel will partner closely with cross-functional teams to support strategic initiatives, provide clear legal guidance, and advise on business and legal risks.

This position is responsible for managing and improving contract lifecycle processes, leveraging tools such as Ironclad and AI-enabled solutions to streamline workflows. The Senior Counsel will also develop and maintain legal templates, playbooks, and scalable processes, while providing counsel to product and technology teams on new features and launches. Advising on data privacy and security matters, particularly in connection with data processing agreements and privacy compliance, is a key aspect of the role.

The ideal candidate holds a J.D. from an accredited law school, is a member of the California bar (or eligible for Registered In-house Counsel status), and brings at least six years of relevant legal experience in both law firm and in-house settings. Demonstrated ability to independently draft and negotiate complex commercial agreements, familiarity with contract lifecycle management systems, and strong communication and organizational skills are essential. Experience supporting product or technology teams, advising on privacy compliance, and working with consumer platforms or marketplace business models is preferred.

Website: www.poshmark.com http://www.poshmark.com

source_jobs.title: Director, Legal – Product & Regulatory @ Kikoff
source_jobs.description:
EXPANDING ACCESS TO FINANCIAL OPPORTUNITY


BUILDING SMARTER, MORE INCLUSIVE CONSUMER FINANCE WITH AI

Kikoff is a high-growth fintech company focused on making financial tools more accessible and affordable. Through AI-driven products, Kikoff helps millions of consumers build credit, reduce debt, and move toward long-term financial stability—particularly those navigating limited access to traditional financial services.

Kikoff is hiring a Director, Legal – Product & Regulatory to serve as a strategic legal partner across product, engineering, and business teams. This role sits at the intersection of fintech innovation and regulation, guiding the development and scaling of consumer financial products within a complex and evolving legal landscape.

In this role, you will advise across Kikoff’s expanding product ecosystem, including credit-building solutions, earned wage access offerings, secured credit cards, and new product initiatives. You will partner closely with product and engineering teams from concept through launch, shaping product design, structure, and go-to-market strategy while ensuring compliance with applicable regulatory frameworks.

You will provide guidance on federal and state consumer financial laws, including TILA, ECOA, FCRA, EFTA, and UDAAP, as well as emerging regulatory considerations around earned wage access and similar products. The role also includes advising on marketing compliance, consumer disclosures, and communications under applicable laws such as TCPA and CAN-SPAM.

A key component of the role involves supporting both bank-partnered and direct-to-consumer product structures, including negotiating partner bank and vendor agreements and advising on regulatory and operational considerations. You will also guide data privacy and consumer data protection efforts, including compliance with frameworks such as CCPA/CPRA and GLBA.

Beyond product counseling, you will monitor regulatory developments from agencies such as the CFPB and state regulators, translating changes into actionable guidance for the business. You will collaborate cross-functionally with compliance, risk, marketing, and operations teams to embed legal guardrails that enable speed without compromising integrity.

The ideal candidate brings deep experience in consumer financial services law, strong product counseling capabilities, and the ability to operate effectively in ambiguous, fast-moving environments. You are a pragmatic advisor who balances legal risk with business outcomes and can communicate complex regulatory concepts clearly to cross-functional stakeholders.

Company Website: kikoff.com http://kikoff.com

source_jobs.title: Sr. Training and Knowledge Management Manager (Legal team) @ Okta
source_jobs.description:
SECURING EVERY IDENTITY IN THE AGE OF AI


BUILDING TRUSTED INFRASTRUCTURE FOR THE FUTURE OF ENTERPRISE

Okta is seeking a Sr. Training & Knowledge Management Manager to lead the vision and strategy for the Legal team’s training and knowledge management program. This is a pivotal role within Okta’s Legal Operations team, responsible for designing and executing a unified, scalable approach to legal knowledge and training—optimized for both human understanding and AI integration.

The Sr. Training & Knowledge Management Manager will develop and implement a multi-year roadmap, partnering across the organization to deliver best-in-class legal training and onboarding. The role involves creating and optimizing legal content for seamless use by AI systems, establishing standards for knowledge capture and governance, and leveraging self-service technologies to empower employees and reduce redundant inquiries. This leader will mentor team members, drive cross-functional collaboration, and define metrics to measure and continually improve program effectiveness.

Ideal candidates bring deep experience in legal training and knowledge management, a strong understanding of legal concepts, and a proven ability to influence and drive change across a global enterprise. Expertise in building engaging content, optimizing for AI, and applying adult learning best practices is essential.

Learn more at: https://www.okta.com

source_jobs.title: Senior Partner, M&A @ VC-Backed Legal Services Startup
source_jobs.description:
LEADING M&A AT THE FRONTIER OF AI-POWERED LEGAL SERVICES
BUILDING THE FUTURE OF HOW DEALS GET DONE

A venture-backed legal services company is seeking a Senior Partner to lead its M&A practice. The company is reimagining how in-house and external legal teams execute transactions — using AI to deliver faster, more efficient deal work at a fraction of the traditional cost. This is an early and defining hire at a well-capitalized firm with a clear vision and an ambitious roadmap.

The Senior Partner will own the M&A practice end-to-end — leading transactions, building client relationships, and helping shape the firm's approach to AI-enabled deal work. This is not a support role. You will be a named partner driving real deals for a growing client base, with the autonomy to build the practice in your own image.

The role is based in New York City and sits at the center of the firm's leadership team.

The ideal candidate brings deep transactional experience — whether from BigLaw, in-house at an acquisitive company, or the buy-side at a fund — and a genuine appetite for building something new. You are energized by the idea of practicing law differently, comfortable operating in an early-stage environment, and ready to bring your deal experience to a platform designed to scale it.

This is a rare opportunity to be a foundational partner at a firm rewriting the rules of legal services, with meaningful equity upside and the chance to define what M&A practice looks like in the AI era.

source_jobs.title: Corporate Counsel, Servicing and Litigation @ Cardless
source_jobs.description:
POWERING CREDIT INNOVATION FOR GLOBAL BRANDS


BUILDING SEAMLESS, AI-DRIVEN CARD PROGRAMS AT SCALE

Cardless is seeking a Corporate Counsel, Servicing and Litigation, to join its growing legal team. Reporting to the General Counsel, this role is central to Cardless’s mission of enabling the world’s largest brands to launch and manage credit card programs with speed and precision. Cardless partners with leading names such as Coinbase, Bilt Rewards, and Alibaba, and operates across all major card networks using advanced AI-powered servicing.

The Corporate Counsel will serve as a cross-functional legal partner, advising on servicing, collections, and litigation matters, while supporting additional legal needs as the business evolves. This high-ownership position is ideal for a structured, detail-oriented attorney with significant experience in consumer finance, who thrives in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

Key responsibilities include ensuring compliance with servicing and collections regulations, managing escalations and litigation, advising on third-party agreements, and optimizing consumer-facing communications. The role also involves leveraging AI and automation to streamline legal workflows and improve operational efficiency.

The ideal candidate holds a J.D. from an accredited law school, is an active member of the bar (New York or California preferred), and brings 8+ years of legal experience, including time at a top-tier law firm and in-house at a technology or fintech company. Demonstrated expertise in servicing, collections, and litigation within consumer finance, strong business judgment, and experience with AI-driven legal processes are essential.

Company Website: https://www.cardless.com

source_jobs.title: Legal AI Product Manager (Attorney) @ The Suite
source_jobs.description:
BUILDING THE FUTURE OF LEGAL AI


EMPOWERING IN-HOUSE COUNSEL WITH PEER-DRIVEN INTELLIGENCE

The Suite is seeking a Legal AI Product Manager (Attorney) to lead the evolution of Lloyd, our community-powered AI co-pilot for in-house legal teams. Lloyd is built on the Braintrust—a decade of peer discussions, vetted templates, outside counsel reviews, and benchmarking data from over 6,000 in-house legal leaders. Unlike other legal AI tools, Lloyd delivers peer-driven insights, answering not just “what do we usually do?” but “what does everyone else like us do?”

In this role, you will be the primary user and architect of Lloyd’s system prompts, diagnosing and improving response quality, identifying content gaps, and rapidly iterating on features in close collaboration with engineering. You will ship code and design directly, run design partner programs, and translate member feedback into actionable product improvements. You will also drive go-to-market efforts, advocate for Lloyd across channels, and ensure the product remains at the forefront of AI advancements.

Ideal candidates have 5–10 years of legal experience spanning both big law and in-house roles, a proven track record of shipping AI skills, agents, or workflows, and a passion for building products that empower legal professionals. Strong written communication, high agency, and a community mindset are essential.

About The Suite:

The Suite builds high-quality, curated professional communities for C-suite executives and their teams, including The L Suite / TechGC and The F Suite. Our flagship platform, the Braintrust (where you are seeing this job post), connects over 5,000 active members from leading tech companies and venture funds, providing real-time peer conversations, research, and insights. We are a well-funded, profitable, and rapidly scaling startup dedicated to helping business leaders excel through trusted, private communities and AI-powered resources.

Company Website: thesuite.com https://thesuite.io

source_jobs.title: Assistant General Counsel, Litigation & Commercial Contracting @ EXL
source_jobs.description:
POWERING DATA-DRIVEN BUSINESS TRANSFORMATION


DELIVERING LEGAL EXCELLENCE FOR GLOBAL DIGITAL OPERATIONS

EXL seeks an accomplished Assistant General Counsel, Litigation & Commercial Contracting, to join its Legal & Compliance team. Reporting to the EVP, General Counsel & Corporate Secretary, and working closely with the SVP, Chief Compliance Officer & Deputy General Counsel - Litigation & Regulated Services, this senior role is central to managing litigation, commercial contracting, and select employment law matters across EXL’s global business lines.

The Assistant General Counsel will oversee the full lifecycle of litigation, including employment disputes, contract claims, and intellectual property matters, coordinating with outside counsel in both U.S. and international jurisdictions. The role involves developing litigation strategies, managing risk exposure, and ensuring cost discipline, while partnering with business operations, HR, and Finance to support discovery and legal hold obligations. The position also requires monitoring regulatory and judicial developments relevant to the business services and technology sectors.

On the employment front, the role provides day-to-day legal counsel to HR and business leaders on hiring, employee relations, performance management, and separations, as well as managing pre-litigation employment claims and related proceedings. The Assistant General Counsel will also advise on employment-related provisions in commercial and vendor agreements.

In commercial contracting, the successful candidate will draft, review, and negotiate complex agreements—including master services, SaaS, NDAs, and vendor contracts—serving as primary legal support to multiple business units. Responsibilities include advising on contractual risk, data privacy, and security requirements, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to mitigate legal exposure at the deal structuring stage. The role also leads post-execution contract management, including renewals, terminations, and dispute escalations.

The ideal candidate holds a J.D. from an accredited law school, is an active member of the bar (New York preferred), and brings 8–12 years of combined experience in commercial litigation and contracting, preferably within technology services, business process management, or professional services. A proven track record in managing complex litigation, negotiating large-scale commercial agreements, and providing clear, business-oriented legal guidance is essential. Experience supporting global operations, familiarity with AI governance or technology licensing, and a background in high-growth, publicly traded environments are preferred.

Company Website: www.exlservice.com http://www.exlservice.com

source_jobs.title: Business Segment GC @ Publicly Traded Company
source_jobs.description:
LEADING LEGAL STRATEGY FOR A $1B GLOBAL BUSINESS SEGMENT


PARTNERING WITH LEADERSHIP AT THE CORE OF A SCALED INDUSTRIAL PLATFORM

A global, publicly traded industrial company is seeking a General Counsel to lead legal strategy for a ~$1B business segment within a ~$2B revenue organization. The company operates across multiple countries, with products embedded in essential industries and a strong track record of operational performance and long-term growth.

This is a broad, hands-on leadership role advising on governance, compliance, commercial matters, and strategic initiatives. The General Counsel will partner closely with executive leadership and operating teams to guide decision-making and support the continued scale of the business.

The role is based at the company’s East Coast headquarters and requires relocation to sit alongside the executive team.

The ideal candidate brings experience in complex operating environments and a practical, business-oriented approach to legal leadership. You are comfortable navigating global operations, managing risk, and supporting a business at scale.

This is a rare opportunity to lead legal for a major business unit within a globally scaled organization, with meaningful impact and long-term leadership potential.

source_jobs.title: Product / Corporate Counsel - Assistant General Counsel @ 1Money
source_jobs.description:
POWERING TRUSTED DIGITAL ASSET INFRASTRUCTURE


ENABLING SECURE, COMPLIANT GLOBAL PAYMENTS

1Money is seeking an accomplished Product / Corporate Counsel – Assistant General Counsel to join its Legal team during a pivotal phase of growth. This high-impact role is central to supporting the development of regulated stablecoin and payments infrastructure worldwide, working closely with senior leadership across product, compliance, finance, and engineering.

The successful candidate will provide expert legal counsel on product development involving stablecoins, payments, blockchain technology, and digital asset services. Responsibilities include advising on complex financial services regulations, BSA/AML/CFT compliance, sanctions, intellectual property, data privacy, and cybersecurity. The role also supports domestic and international licensing, regulatory strategy, and direct engagement with global regulators.

Key duties include drafting and negotiating sophisticated commercial agreements, supporting corporate governance and venture financing, and translating evolving regulatory requirements into actionable business guidance. The position requires leading multiple high-stakes legal projects in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment, collaborating cross-functionally, and managing outside counsel.

Candidates should have a JD from a top-tier ABA-accredited law school, active U.S. bar membership, and 5-7 years of relevant legal experience spanning large law firm and in-house roles within financial services or digital assets. Deep expertise in financial regulation, commercial transactions, and corporate governance is essential. Experience with stablecoin issuance, digital asset custody, or blockchain infrastructure is a plus.

Company Website: https://1money.com

source_jobs.title: Commercial Counsel @ Sentry
source_jobs.description:
EMPOWERING DEVELOPERS TO BUILD BETTER SOFTWARE


SETTING THE STANDARD FOR AI-NATIVE APPLICATION MONITORING

Sentry seeks a Commercial Counsel to join its legal team, supporting the company’s mission to help developers fix errors and performance issues before users notice. Trusted by over 200,000 organizations, Sentry is building the future of application monitoring with AI at its core.

The Commercial Counsel will structure, draft, and negotiate a wide range of commercial agreements, including customer, channel partner, technology supplier, and marketing contracts. This role partners closely with sales, customer success, security, compliance, finance, and marketing to ensure efficient and effective legal support for commercial transactions. The position also provides strategic legal and business advice on commercial, regulatory, data protection, and intellectual property matters, and explores opportunities to leverage AI to scale legal processes.

Ideal candidates bring 3-5 years of relevant legal experience, including at least 2 years focused on technology transactions, a deep understanding of cloud and software technology, and a proven ability to balance legal protections with business velocity. Strong communication, negotiation skills, and a collaborative approach are essential, as is the ability to explain complex contract provisions to internal partners. A J.D. from an accredited law school and California bar membership are required.

Sentry is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team, ensuring equal opportunity for all employees and candidates.

Company Website: https://sentry.io

source_jobs.title: Corporate Counsel @ Oklo
source_jobs.description:
DELIVERING CLEAN, RELIABLE ENERGY FOR THE FUTURE


ADVANCING FAST FISSION POWER AND NUCLEAR INNOVATION

Oklo is seeking a Senior Counsel, Corporate to join its Legal team and play a pivotal role in supporting the company’s rapid growth and mission to deliver advanced fission power plants and clean energy solutions. This role is ideal for a junior-to-mid-level corporate attorney with 3–5 years of experience who thrives in dynamic, high-growth environments and is eager to contribute across a broad range of legal and business needs.

The Senior Counsel, Corporate will support public company legal work, including SEC filings, public disclosures, NYSE compliance, capital markets and financing transactions, annual meeting preparation, and M&A diligence and negotiations. The role also involves routine corporate governance, subsidiary management, commercial contracting, and providing practical legal research and advice to diverse business teams. Reporting to the Associate General Counsel, this position requires agility, sound judgment, and a willingness to learn and adapt in a fast-paced setting.

Candidates should have a J.D. from an ABA-accredited law school, admission to practice in at least one U.S. state, and experience supporting public companies with SEC filings, governance, and strategic transactions. Experience in highly regulated industries, especially energy or infrastructure, is valued. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, a strong communicator, and comfortable managing multiple priorities while driving projects to completion.

Oklo values collaboration, humility, ownership, and pathfinding. The company is committed to building a diverse team that works together to solve complex challenges and deliver on the promise of clean, affordable energy.

Company Website: https://www.oklo.com

source_jobs.title: Associate General Counsel, Corporate and Securities @ Hut 8 Corp.
source_jobs.description:
BUILDING THE FUTURE OF DIGITAL INFRASTRUCTURE


POWERING NEXT-GENERATION COMPUTING AT SCALE

Hut 8 is seeking an Associate General Counsel, Corporate and Securities, to lead public company reporting, corporate governance, and capital markets activities at the forefront of technology, energy, and infrastructure. This high-impact role offers significant ownership of core public company functions and direct involvement in strategic growth, financing, and governance initiatives as Hut 8 continues to scale its data center operations for AI, Colocation, Cloud, and Bitcoin Mining.

The Associate General Counsel will oversee the SEC reporting lifecycle, ensuring accuracy and compliance across all 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, proxy, and related securities filings. The role partners closely with the Chief Legal Officer, executive leadership, and the Board of Directors on governance, disclosure, and Nasdaq compliance, while helping to build and scale the company’s public company infrastructure. Responsibilities include leading legal support for capital markets and financing transactions, managing governance processes and documentation, and collaborating cross-functionally on broader corporate, financing, and securities matters.

Ideal candidates bring substantial experience advising public companies on SEC reporting, disclosure obligations, corporate governance, and capital markets transactions, with a blend of top-tier law firm training and in-house experience. The role requires strong business and legal judgment, the ability to operate independently in a dynamic environment, and effective communication with executive leadership and cross-functional teams.

Hut 8 offers the opportunity to work alongside driven peers from diverse backgrounds, contribute to new products and initiatives, and build a lifelong network at the intersection of technology, energy, and infrastructure.

Company Website: https://hut8.com

source_jobs.title: Associate Legal Counsel @ UVeye
source_jobs.description:
REDEFINING VEHICLE SAFETY WITH AI INNOVATION


BUILDING THE FUTURE OF AUTOMATED AUTOMOTIVE INSPECTIONS

UVeye is seeking an Associate Legal Counsel to support its mission of transforming vehicle safety and reliability worldwide. As a pioneer in AI-driven vehicle inspection systems, UVeye partners with leading automotive and technology companies to deliver advanced solutions across manufacturing, dealerships, fleets, and more.

In this role, the Associate Legal Counsel will work closely with senior legal leadership and cross-functional teams to support UVeye’s expanding global operations. Responsibilities include drafting, reviewing, and negotiating a broad range of commercial agreements—such as SaaS, licensing, service, vendor, and partnership contracts—while progressively taking ownership of defined agreement types. The role also involves supporting compliance initiatives related to contracts, privacy, and AI governance, managing contract workflows, and contributing to the development of contract templates and legal best practices.

The Associate Legal Counsel will provide practical legal guidance to teams across Sales, Procurement, Product, Operations, HR, R&D, and Finance, and assist with corporate governance, compliance, and risk management. Additional duties include conducting legal research on regulatory and industry developments, supporting dispute prevention, and helping to maintain internal policies and training materials.

Candidates should hold a Juris Doctor from an accredited law school, be an active member of at least one U.S. state bar (New York or New Jersey preferred), and have at least one year of professional legal experience with exposure to commercial contracts. Strong drafting, organizational, and communication skills are essential, along with a keen interest in technology and scaling legal infrastructure in a dynamic environment.

Learn more about UVeye: https://uveye.com

source_jobs.title: Product Counsel, ERISA & Operations @ Human Interest
source_jobs.description:
ENSURING ACCESS TO RETIREMENT FOR ALL


TRANSFORMING HOW SMALL BUSINESSES SECURE FINANCIAL FUTURES

Human Interest seeks a Product Counsel, ERISA & Operations to serve as the in-house ERISA expert, guiding the company’s mission to expand retirement plan access for small and mid-sized businesses. This role sits at the intersection of ERISA, technology, and operations, supporting both customer-facing and product teams as Human Interest builds a modern, accessible 401(k) recordkeeping platform.

The Product Counsel will provide real-time, practical legal guidance on ERISA, IRC, and fiduciary matters, ensuring operational compliance and translating complex regulatory requirements into actionable, plain-language advice. Responsibilities include advising on plan sponsor and participant questions, reviewing disclosures and communications for compliance, and supporting the product team with subject matter expertise as new features are developed. The role requires deep ERISA expertise, strong business judgment, and fluency with AI tools in legal workflows.

Ideal candidates bring 5–8 years of substantive ERISA experience focused on qualified retirement plans, including at least two years in a law firm setting, and a proven ability to advise non-legal teams in fast-paced environments. Experience with operational compliance, fiduciary rules, and product or technology-driven legal issues is essential. Admission to the bar and a collaborative, cross-functional approach are required.

Join Human Interest to help close the retirement gap and shape the future of financial security for millions of employees.

Company Website: https://humaninterest.com

source_jobs.title: Commercial and Privacy Counsel - EMEA @ Constructor
source_jobs.description:
POWERING INTELLIGENT PRODUCT DISCOVERY FOR GLOBAL COMMERCE


ENABLING PRIVACY-FIRST AI ACROSS THE EMEA DIGITAL ECONOMY

Constructor is building the infrastructure behind modern e-commerce discovery—powering search, browse, recommendations, and personalization for leading global retailers. By combining AI with real-time behavioral data, Constructor enables more relevant, performant, and personalized shopping experiences at scale.

Constructor is hiring a Commercial and Privacy Counsel – EMEA to support its rapidly growing international business. As the second member of the legal team, this role operates at the center of commercial execution, product development, and regulatory strategy—partnering closely with Sales, Product, Engineering, and leadership to support expansion across European markets.

In this role, you will lead commercial legal support for EMEA customer relationships, drafting and negotiating enterprise SaaS agreements, including MSAs, DPAs, and AI-related terms. You will work directly with go-to-market teams to structure deals, balance risk, and enable efficient revenue growth across diverse jurisdictions.

You will also serve as a key advisor on European data protection and privacy frameworks, including GDPR, the ePrivacy Directive, NIS2, and emerging regulations such as the EU AI Act. This includes overseeing elements of the company’s data protection compliance program—supporting DPIAs, ROPA maintenance, DSARs, and broader privacy-by-design initiatives across product and engineering workflows.

The role extends into forward-looking regulatory strategy, where you will monitor evolving AI and data regulations across the EU and UK, translate requirements into practical guidance, and help shape compliance efforts as new frameworks take effect. You will also contribute to building scalable legal infrastructure, including templates, playbooks, and self-service tools that improve operational efficiency across a growing international organization.

The ideal candidate brings strong experience in European data protection law and commercial technology transactions, with the ability to operate autonomously, prioritize effectively, and deliver clear, business-oriented guidance in a fast-paced environment.

Company Website: constructor.io http://constructor.io

source_jobs.title: Product Counsel @ Fivetran
source_jobs.description:
UNLOCKING THE POWER OF TRUSTED DATA


ENABLING SEAMLESS DATA MOVEMENT AND TRANSFORMATION FOR THE MODERN ENTERPRISE

Fivetran is seeking a Product Attorney to join its Legal team, supporting the mission to make access to data as simple and reliable as electricity. As Product Counsel, you will provide practical, solution-oriented legal advice throughout the product development lifecycle, partnering closely with Product, Engineering, Privacy, and Go-to-Market teams. This role is central to enabling innovation while managing risk across Fivetran’s evolving suite of data and analytics solutions.

Reporting to the Director - Legal Product & Strategy, you will advise on a broad range of legal issues, including intellectual property, software licensing, privacy, AI, security, and regulatory considerations. You will review new products and features, develop scalable legal guidance and training, and collaborate with cross-functional legal colleagues to address complex, cross-cutting matters. Success in this role requires strong legal judgment, business acumen, and the ability to translate complex legal concepts into actionable guidance for technical and business stakeholders.

The ideal candidate brings a J.D., active U.S. state bar membership, and 4-7 years of relevant legal experience counseling SaaS companies. Experience advising on open-source software licensing, compliance, and governance, as well as leveraging AI tools to enhance legal workflows, is highly valued. This is an opportunity to shape the legal foundation of a company at the forefront of data infrastructure, working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment that values diversity, equity, and inclusion.

Company Website: https://fivetran.com

source_jobs.title: Corporate Counsel @ Emerald AI

ENABLING DATA CENTERS TO POWER THE FUTURE RESPONSIBLY

Emerald AI is seeking a Corporate Counsel to play a pivotal role in supporting the company’s legal needs as it drives innovation at the intersection of AI and energy. As part of a cross-functional team, the Corporate Counsel will partner closely with business, product, engineering, and leadership to provide timely, practical legal guidance that enables rapid and responsible growth.

This role is embedded within the business, advising on commercial agreements, product and regulatory matters, and contributing to strategic initiatives. The Corporate Counsel will help develop foundational legal processes, templates, and systems to ensure legal operations scale alongside the company. Key responsibilities include drafting and negotiating agreements, advising on data usage, privacy, and IP, supporting corporate governance, and staying current on relevant regulatory frameworks.

The ideal candidate holds a JD with active bar membership, has at least 3 years of legal experience in commercial contracts and corporate matters, and is adept at working cross-functionally with technical and business teams. Experience in technology, AI/ML, or infrastructure sectors, and familiarity with data privacy or regulated industries, is preferred.
